summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff said in arecent interview that AI now does up to 50% of all work at the company . In May, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said 20% to 30% of the tech giant’s code is now written by AI coding assistants .  The metrics cited seem precise, yet when asked, their spokespeople declined to provide any details about how the numbers were calculated or how they defined the work that they claim AI has done . “We don’t yet have a common framework for measuring what ‘percent of work’ really means in the age of AI,” said Malvika Jethmalani, founder of human capital advisory firm Atvis Group .  Salesforce CEO describes AI's ability to do up to half the work at his company as a “digital labor revolution” Benioff didn’t clarify what “work” means in this context . He mentioned using AI to co-author Salesforce’s corporate plan, but did not detail what that plan was or how much AI contributed .  CEO Benioff said that AI technology is already being used as employees within an organization . The flex can even cause anxiety among employees who hear it as “we're automating you out,” an occupational psychologist said .  Research from firms likeGartners says many AI-driven projects are likely to fail by 2027 because their value is unclear . Companies also must deal with the disconnect between what C-suite executives say about AI and what’s actually happening on the ground .  CEOs may be using this flex as little more than a boast to competitors, Bester said . CEOs are saying “just look at us, we are ahead of the curve on this,” he said . For now, CEOs “obviously want to show their stakeholders that they are on board with AI”  Klarna CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowskireversedan AI-driven hiring freeze and announced the company is adding more human staff . Klarna is now hiring to ensure customers always have the option to speak with a real person .

Microsoft plans to eliminate nearly 4% of its global workforce . The move follows earlier layoffs in May that affected 6,000 employees . Microsoft has earmarked $80 billion for fiscal year 2025, much of it aimed at scaling AI and cloud computing capabilities .  The company said the layoffs will help flatten organizational layers, reduce management complexity and simplify product and process workflows . Microsoft isn't alone. Meta (META,Financials), Alphabet's Google (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) have all made job cuts .

Cantor Fitzgerald urged investors to buy unprofitable and near-revenue-less quantum computing company Rigetti today . Quantum computing could have "enormous economical implications," but that doesn't mean quantum stocks like Rigetti will be profitable . Rigetti is a small fish in this still-small pond, sharing the water with megalodons likeIBM,Intel, andMicrosoft .  Rigetti trades at a "steep" multiple to "any near-term revenue or earnings forecast" Cantor itself confides: "We are likely years away from full-scale quantum capabilities" Most analysts don't see the company turning profitable, ever -- or at least not before 2030 .  The Motley Fool’s total average return is 1,045% — a market-crushing outperformance compared to178% for the S&P 500 .
